data.content:
## Incremental Build Analysis

Both custom targets in `IncrementalBroken.csproj` break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** → MSBuild always re-runs it.
- Uses `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` — a **volatile value** that changes every build, so the written file always has new content.
- The modified file timestamp can invalidate downstream up-to-date checks.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** → MSBuild always re-runs it.
- Rewrites `git-hash.g.cs` on every build, even when the git hash hasn't changed. This **changes the file's modification time**, forcing `CoreCompile` to re-run.
- The `<Compile Include=...>` is also re-evaluated every time.

### Net effect
Even with zero source changes, every `dotnet build` triggers a full recompile because these targets mutate compilation inputs unconditionally.

### Recommended fixes
- **`GenerateTimestamp`**: Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and use a hash or sentinel to avoid rewriting when content hasn't changed. Better yet, consider whether a volatile timestamp truly needs to be in every build.
- **`EmbedGitHash`**: Add `Inputs="$(ProjectDir).git/HEAD;$(ProjectDir).git/refs/heads/**"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"`, and use a diff-then-write pattern so the file is only touched when the hash actually changes.
data.reasoningText:
I can see two custom targets with incremental build issues. Let me analyze them:

1. **`GenerateTimestamp`** (line 6-11): This target writes the current timestamp to a file on every build. It has:
   - No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes → always runs
   - Uses `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` which is volatile — changes every build
   - This causes `build-timestamp.txt` to be modified every build, which triggers downstream targets

2. **`EmbedGitHash`** (line 13-23): This target runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes a generated .cs file. It has:
   - No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes → always runs
   - Even if the git hash hasn't changed, it rewrites the file, modifying the timestamp
   - This modifies `git-hash.g.cs` every build, which triggers CoreCompile to re-run

Both targets run before CoreCompile and lack the metadata MSBuild needs to skip them, so they always execute and modify compilation inputs, forcing CoreCompile to rebuild unnecessarily.
